name: Tajos de los Bermejales Loop
name_original: Cañón Río Cacín — desde Moraleda de Zafayona
description: This difficult 35.7-mile road cycling route from Moraleda de Zafayona takes you through the dramatic Río Cacín canyon.
region_intro: Road cycling around Moraleda De Zafayona offers varied terrain and challenging ascents within the province of Granada, Spain. The region is characterized by an agricultural tapestry of expansive fields, ancient olive groves, and traditional orchards along the Río Cacín. Cyclists can expect considerable elevation gains, with routes often featuring a mix of paved and unpaved sections. The landscape provides panoramic views of the fertile Vega de Granada and glimpses of the Sierra Tejeda. Best road cycling routes around Moraleda De Zafayona The most popular road cycling route is Tajos de los Bermejales Loop, a 35.7 miles (57.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbing through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tower of Huétor-Tájar – River loop from Tocón, a moderate 29.7 miles (47.8 km) path. This route follows the river, offering views of the surrounding agricultural areas. Local road cyclists also love the View of Olive Groves – Montefrío Castle loop from Huétor Tájar, a 45.8 miles (73.8 km) trail leading through extensive olive groves, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Moraleda De Zafayona is defined by agricultural fields, olive groves, and river valleys, with distant views of mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Moraleda De Zafayona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Moraleda De Zafayona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Moraleda De Zafayona?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Moraleda De Zafayona, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 50 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Moraleda De Zafayona?
A: The region offers varied terrain, characterized by an agricultural tapestry of expansive fields, ancient olive groves, and traditional orchards along the Río Cacín. Cyclists should anticipate considerable elevation gains, with some routes featuring a mix of paved and unpaved sections. You'll also find panoramic views of the fertile Vega de Granada and glimpses of the Sierra Tejeda.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant climbing, there are options for all skill levels. You can find 2 easy routes and 20 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Huétor-Tájar – River loop from Tocón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 47.8 km path following the river through agricultural areas.

Q: What are the more challenging road cycling routes like in this region?
A: For experienced cyclists, there are 25 difficult routes with significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38287122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Moclín – Castle of Íllora loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 100 km with almost 1,500 meters of climbing, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Moraleda De Zafayona?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983206332/tajos-de-los-bermejales-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajos de los Bermejales Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular example, offering a 57.5 km ride with significant climbing through the local landscape.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes offer stunning views of the fertile Vega de Granada, expansive fields, and ancient olive groves. You can also encounter the Río Cacín, which has sculpted the land, and distinctive geological formations like cliffs. For a notable viewpoint, consider exploring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5038628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On monte, on descend et on recommence&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2575442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of the Río Cacín Canal&lt;/a&gt;, which transports water from the Bermejales reservoir. Other points of interest include the Bridge over the Río Cacín and the historical Iglesia Parroquial de San Marcos in the village.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Moraleda De Zafayona?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling trailheads?
A: While specific parking areas for every trailhead are not detailed, Moraleda De Zafayona is a village, and parking can typically be found within or on the outskirts of the village and nearby towns like Huétor Tájar, which serve as common starting points for many routes. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small villages and towns where you can find local cafes and establishments to refuel. The region is known for its rural charm, so you'll often have opportunities to experience local hospitality and cuisine during your ride.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling options in Moraleda De Zafayona?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier and moderate options that could be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with some climbing. The 2 easy routes and 20 moderate routes provide a good starting point for exploring less demanding terrain.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes around Moraleda De Zafayona?
A: The road cycling routes in Moraleda De Zafayona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, the breathtaking scenery of olive groves and agricultural fields, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents that define the region's cycling experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers several long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10300843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Huétor-Tájar – View of Alhama de Granada loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt; is an 81.8 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Olive Groves – Montefrío Castle loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt; spans 73.8 km, both offering extensive rides through the diverse landscape.
tips: Uphill or downhill from here you can see the town of Cacín and where the Río Cacín formed an alluvial terrace that gives a difference in height between the fluvial space and from the top of the road.

It is not a Roman bridge but for that it is still a great construction, one of the most important in the area. It was built at the beginning of the 20th century. It soon fell into disuse, at the time he built the Pantano de los Bermejales, which began to channel traffic.
Moraleda de Zafayona is a town located in the geographical center called Vega de Granada, at the foot of the Río Alhama and the Río Cacín.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Cañón Río Cacín cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as difficult and requires a very good fitness level. It involves significant elevation changes over its 57.5 km length, making it a demanding ride for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads suitable for road cycling. However, be prepared for undulating terrain and significant ascents, as it's known as a 'hard road ride' with considerable climbing.

faqs.question: What scenic spots or landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the dramatic landscape of the Río Cacín canyon, characterized by impressive geological formations and towering cliffs (Tajos). Notable points include the On monte, on descend et on recommence viewpoint and the Cacín Bridge (Cacín River Canyon). The route also passes through the settlement of Moraleda de Zafayona.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are generally the best times. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter, making the demanding climbs more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Moraleda de Zafayona. While specific designated cyclist parking isn't detailed, public parking options should be available within the town. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Can I reach Moraleda de Zafayona by public transport with my bike?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Moraleda de Zafayona, such as ALSA buses from Granada, may be available. However, policies regarding bicycle transport can vary, so it's crucial to check with the specific bus operator (e.g., ALSA) in advance for their current rules and any associated fees for carrying bikes.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this road route through the Cañón Río Cacín from Moraleda de Zafayona.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog on a leash on a demanding road route like this is not recommended for safety reasons, both for the dog and the cyclist. There are no specific provisions for dogs on this type of trail.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Moraleda de Zafayona, where you can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. As the route traverses a natural canyon, facilities may be sparse along the main cycling path, so plan to stock up on supplies before you start.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of official trails such as 'Tajos de los Bermejales', 'Carretera de Cacín al Pantano', and 'Camino del Turro a Cacín'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given its difficulty and length, bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling gear, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.
